question.content: A square loop abcd slides on a smooth incline with angle θ through two magnetic regions I and II, where the magnetic fields have equal magnitude and are perpendicular to the incline plane. When side cd enters region I, the loop speed is v and remains v until side ab leaves region I. Determine the potential difference across side cd at the instant when side cd enters region I. Answer format: a single algebraic expression; write only the expression, with no explanatory text or equals sign.

step_1.content: At the entry instant of region I, only side cd cuts magnetic field lines, so the motional emf magnitude is E=Blv.
step_1.key_point: Use the local emf on side cd at entry.

step_3.content: The loop is uniform, so each side resistance is R/4. Current is I=E/R, and terminal voltage across cd is U_cd=E-I*(R/4).
step_3.key_point: U_cd equals source emf minus internal drop of side cd.
